reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement component by component. "Odd primes" is encoded as `p.Prime ∧ p > 2` and `q.Prime ∧ q > 2`, which is equivalent since 2 is the only even prime. "Distinct" becomes `p ≠ q`. "$p-1$ divides $q-1$" becomes `p - 1 ∣ q - 1`; natural-number truncated subtraction is fine here because `p, q > 2` ensures `p, q ≥ 3`. The coprimality `(n, pq) = 1` is encoded as `n.gcd (p*q) = 1`, and the conclusion `n^{q-1} ≡ 1 (pq)` is `n^(q-1) ≡ 1 [MOD p*q]`. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure is preserved.
